_README: LLM-derived 0–3 axis scores for fruit liqueurs (axes: sweet, citrus, orchard, berry, floral, tropical, spice). `orchard` covers tree fruits (cherry, apricot, apple, pear, sloe). `spice` is the critical axis distinguishing complex spiced cordials (BroVo Boomerang) from pure single-fruit liqueurs (Luxardo Maraschino). Re-score after tasting.
_design_notes: Note the key Maraschino comparison: Luxardo Maraschino is a pure marasca cherry distillate (orchard=3, spice=0), while BroVo Boomerang adds cinnamon, walnut, orange, peppercorn (orchard=3, spice=3, citrus=1). They're not substitutes; the matcher will surface this when a recipe expects one vs the other.

182.name: Luxardo Maraschino
182.reasoning: Marasca cherries infused 3 yr, copper-pot distilled, ash-wood aged since 1821. Pure marasca character — bitter cherry pit + nuttiness. The benchmark single-fruit liqueur. NO spice, NO citrus.

172.name: broVo Boomerang
172.reasoning: Spiced cherry cordial by Micah Melton (The Aviary, Chicago). Cherry + apricot + walnut + cinnamon + orange + vanilla + peppercorn. Functionally distinct from Maraschino — orchard + spice + slight citrus. Not a Maraschino substitute despite the shared shelf position.

510.name: Cherry Heering
510.reasoning: Danish cherry liqueur (1818) — Stevens cherries + spice blend, oak-aged. Sweeter and more spice-touched than Maraschino, fuller body. Used in Singapore Sling, Blood and Sand, Remember the Maine.

562.name: Crème de Violette
562.reasoning: Pure violet floral liqueur. The Aviation cocktail's defining ingredient. Sweet + floral, nothing else.

155.name: Gabriel Boudier Crème de Cassis
155.reasoning: PGI-certified Crème de Cassis from Noir de Bourgogne blackcurrants (1874). Cold-macerated. Pure blackcurrant — the berry axis archetype.

177.name: Greenbar Distillery Hibiscus Liqueur
177.reasoning: Organic hibiscus + California lemon zest, 96 pts Wine Enthusiast. Hibiscus is floral with tart berry undertones; lemon zest adds citrus.

180.name: La Grande Passion
180.reasoning: Passion fruit liqueur with XO Armagnac (10yr) base — discontinued 1994 so a rarity. Passion fruit's bright tropical character with slight citrus brightness.

181.name: Luxardo Apricot Albicocca
181.reasoning: Apricot pulp infusion since 1935. The apricot equivalent of Maraschino — pure single-fruit liqueur, no spice complexity.

512.name: Midori Melon Liqueur
512.reasoning: Japanese honeydew melon liqueur. Sweet + bright tropical, nothing else. The bright green color is unmistakable.

232.name: Nouvelle Wild Harvest Strawberry Schnapps
232.reasoning: American-style strawberry schnapps. Sweet + strawberry-forward. Strawberry sits in the berry axis.

186.name: Skunk Brothers Sweet 45 Cherry Cordial
186.reasoning: Washington cherry cordial by veteran-owned craft distillery. Cherry-forward, sweet, less complex than Cherry Heering.

220.name: Ford's Sloe Gin
220.reasoning: Higher-proof sloe gin (29% ABV) made with Ford's base gin + French/English sloe fruit steeped 12 weeks. Sloe is small blackthorn fruit — plum/cherry adjacent + tart berry character. Cleaner and more juniper-tinged than typical sloe gins.

488.name: St-Germain Elderflower Liqueur
488.reasoning: French elderflower liqueur, fresh elderflower blossoms hand-picked once a year. Tasting: delicate floral with notes of pear, citrus, tropical fruit. Elderflower is the dominant character (floral); pear hint goes to orchard; mild citrus and tropical bring complexity.

196.name: Cointreau
196.reasoning: Premium French triple sec (40% ABV) from sweet + bitter orange peels since 1875. Drier and more refined than generic triple sec — concentrated orange oil. The benchmark citrus liqueur.

566.name: Triple Sec
566.reasoning: Generic triple sec (15-25% ABV) — sweeter and less concentrated than Cointreau. BA description: 'most common style of curaçao... typically 15-25% ABV and sweeter than premium versions like Cointreau.'

499.name: Kijafa Danish Cherry Wine
499.reasoning: Danish cherry wine (16% ABV — fortified-wine strength, not spirit-strength). Sweet cherry with spice notes — softer body than a cherry liqueur but in the same flavor neighborhood.

136.name: Skunk Brothers Sweet Apple Pie
136.reasoning: Apple brandy + brown sugar + honey + cinnamon + cloves + pie spices, with a cinnamon stick in each bottle. 42.5% ABV — high-proof spiced apple cordial. Apple → orchard 3; the spice load is the defining feature (cinnamon-cloves-spices) → spice 3.
